summary: Neurologists/neurosurgeons have exclusive authorization under law due to their specialized knowledge; a treating physician lacks objectivity as part of ongoing medical treatment responsibilities leading them unsuitable per legal guidelines set forth by the act regarding declaration procedures involving potential organ donation scenarios post-brainstem determination. Agent1 concludes based primarily on professional specialization versus inherent conflicts with direct caregiving duties when it comes to making these critical assessments related to life-ending conditions like brainstem death certification necessary before considering transplant options legally permissible within specified regulatory frameworks governing human organs transplantation
